records.ety: [F., fr. L. pensio a paying, payment, fr. pendere, pensum, to weight, to pay; akin to pendere to hang. See Pendant, and cf. Spend.]

defs: A payment; a tribute; something paid or given.
quotes: The stomach's pension, and the time's expense.

defs: A stated allowance to a person in consideration of past services; payment made to one retired from service, on account of age, disability, or other cause; also, a regular stipend paid by a government to retired public officers, disabled soldiers, the families of soldiers killed in service, or to meritorious authors, or the like.
quotes: To all that kept the city pensions and wages.

defs: A certain sum of money paid to a clergyman in lieu of tithes.
A boarding house or boarding school in France, Belgium, Switzerland, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pensioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Pensioning.]

defs: To grant a pension to; to pay a regular stipend to; in consideration of service already performed; -- sometimes followed by off; as, to pension off a servant.
quotes: One knighted Blackmore, and one pensioned Quarles.
